articles.title: Iran deal might bring some relief at the pump, but pre-war prices won't return overnight
articles.description: Falling gas prices might get a tailwind from Iran ceasefire deal, but experts warned that oil production and exports from the Middle East won't return to normal overnight.

articles.title: Market Soars as US-Iran Peace Deal Calms Inflation Fears
articles.description: Major stock indexes soared and bond prices rallied after a preliminary US-Iran agreement aimed at ending Gulf tensions was announced. This peace initiative, which also triggered a drop in oil prices, is likely to ease inflationary pressures and lessen the necessity for higher interest rates globally.

articles.title: Potential End of War Tests Trump’s Promise of Quick Economic Rebound
articles.description: Gas prices and other goods could remain elevated for months, adding to the political challenge facing the White House in the midterm elections.

articles.title: Thai fuel prices to stay high until Q4
articles.description: Domestic oil prices in Thailand are expected to remain elevated until the final quarter of this year, even though global crude benchmarks have eased and the US is set to sign a peace deal with Iran this Friday to end nearly four months of conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: Lingering questions from Iran deal for oil and gas prices
articles.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has displaced tens of millions of barrels of crude since the war began in February.

articles.title: Stocks Leap Worldwide, And Oil Prices Drop After U.S. And Iran Reach Tentative Deal On War
articles.description: Stock markets rallied worldwide, and oil prices eased after the United States and Iran reached a tentative deal to get the global flow of crude going again.
